input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.content:
`.testsettings` files are **not supported** by modern MSTest (v3+). You need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file. Let me create the equivalent configuration.

Your `.testsettings` configures three things:
1. **Assembly resolution** (load context) — handled automatically in SDK-style projects
2. **Test timeout** (30 seconds) — now set via `[Timeout]` attribute or `<MSTest>` runsettings node
3. **Code coverage data collector** — migrated to the `<DataCollectors>` runsettings node

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <MSTest>
    <!-- Global test timeout: 30 000 ms (was TestTimeout in .testsettings) -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- DeploymentEnabled=false is the modern default; set true only if you
         rely on [DeploymentItem] to copy files into the test run directory -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.SingleFileCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage>
            <!-- Adjust include/exclude filters as needed -->
            <ModulePaths>
              <Exclude>
                <ModulePath>.*tests.*\.dll$</ModulePath>
              </Exclude>
            </ModulePaths>
          </CodeCoverage>
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.reasoningText:
The user has an MSTest v3 project with a `.testsettings` file. The `.testsettings` format is legacy and not supported by modern MSTest. They need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file.

Let me invoke the migrate-mstest-v1v2-to-v3 skill since it covers replacing .testsettings with .runsettings.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <MSTest>
    <!-- Global test timeout: 30 000 ms (was TestTimeout in .testsettings) -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- DeploymentEnabled=false is the modern default; set true only if you
         rely on [DeploymentItem] to copy files into the test run directory -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.SingleFileCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage>
            <!-- Adjust include/exclude filters as needed -->
            <ModulePaths>
              <Exclude>
                <ModulePath>.*tests.*\.dll$</ModulePath>
              </Exclude>
            </ModulePaths>
          </CodeCoverage>
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content: Now wire the `.runsettings` file into the project so it's picked up automatically:

Here's a summary of the migration:

| `.testsettings` setting | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `<Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000" />` | `<MSTest><TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout></MSTest>` |
| `AssemblyResolution` / `useLoadContext` | No longer needed (SDK-style projects handle this) |
| Code Coverage `DataCollector` (uri v1.0) | `DataCollector` under `<DataCollectionRunSettings>` (uri v2.0) |
| Deployment config | `<DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>` (default) |

**What changed:**
- Created `local.runsettings` with equivalent settings
- Updated `.csproj` to reference it via `<RunSettingsFilePath>`
- The old `local.testsettings` can be deleted once verified

Run tests with: `dotnet test` (the runsettings is picked up automatically), or explicitly: `dotnet test --settings local.runsettings`.
